message:
The other day, I decided to take my dog, Max, to the park for some fresh air. You know, the kind of day where the sun is shining, and the breeze is just right. Instead of grabbing his usual squeaky toy, I brought along an old frisbee I found in the garage. 

We spent hours tossing it back and forth—well, mostly me chasing it down because he’s still figuring out the whole ‘fetch’ concept. But watching him dart across the grass, tail wagging like a little helicopter, reminded me of how much fun we can have without all the bells and whistles of modern toys. 

It got me thinking about preparedness, too. Sometimes, the simplest things can bring us the most joy and connection—like knowing how to enjoy a day outdoors without relying on gadgets. Plus, it’s a great reminder that being prepared doesn’t always mean being high-tech! 

Have you rediscovered any low-tech fun lately?
